publication_status.detail: Civis PR #1383 was open when scored; origin branch head was 11718858f8aa062c7ad12ecc0faac875b780ec70, not local bf5caf9.

method: 80-pillar qualitative 0-3 + registry 30+41 formal bands; evidence-only delta

changed_pillars.evidence: README points to registry/audits/.github spine and states Civis owns FR-CIV-* godgame work only.
docs/THREAT_MODEL.md covers assets, boundaries, STRIDE-lite threats, current mitigations, and gaps.
Seed threat model exists; it is not yet a reviewed STRIDE table or attack tree.
The latest PR cargo-deny check fails on unlicensed workspace crates, unapproved git sources, unmaintained bincode, and RUSTSEC-2026-0204.

non_awards.reason: 35-phase order, 37 Live verbs, terraform_extent, and legends improve completion but do not make partial L15 complete; Bevy polish is still partial.
Compile gate and quality manifest passed, but cargo-deny failed and governance checks were not clean.
cargo-deny now executes but fails policy/advisory checks; its schema repair alone does not improve security maturity.
civis-3d-verify runs clippy without -D warnings and engine FR integration coverage retains ignored/stubbed tests.
No evidence of OTEL, emitted operational metrics, SLOs, alert routing, dashboards, or readiness checks.

ECS state, save-db, replay; incomplete campaign paths
tracing and health surface; no OTEL

README declares godgame identity and org spine pointers
README, justfile, devcontainer; contributor flow remains partial
JSON-RPC errors and tracing; client UX incomplete

cargo-deny gate and scheduled audit; no clean per-PR audit/SARIF proof
Domain metrics are not operational metrics
just/process-compose setup; no verified seed-data path
MCP integration tests, not a multi-environment agent-eval pipeline
FR matrices and ADRs; no org eval policy

remaining_priority: Replace engine stubs and restore meaningful FR integration coverage.
Require clippy -D warnings and preserve all-target test quality.
Resolve documented high RUSTSECs and make scheduled audit green.
Add OTEL, readiness, SLOs, alerting, and dashboards.
Add AuthN, per-RPC limits, and reviewed STRIDE/attack-tree modeling.
